course_description: This course provides an exploration of psychological disorders, with a strong focus on diagnostic systems, assessment methods, and understanding of treatment approaches. Through both theoretical and applied learning, students will develop a thorough and critical understanding of widely used classificatory systems, and gain hands-on experience in clinical interviewing, mental status examinations, and diagnostic assessments when working with adults. Special emphasis is placed on the biopsychosocial approach to diagnosis, case formulation and treatment planning, considering developmental, cultural, and ethical factors. Throughout the course, students will engage in critical analysis of the research evidence-base, fostering a reflexive and evidence-informed approach to their professional development.

course_aims:
This course aims to provide students with:

(a) a working knowledge of descriptive psychopathology, basic nosology, and current theories of major psychological disorders, and

(b) a set of skills for assessing, describing, and formulating adult psychopathology.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply and critically evaluate diagnostic classification systems and their role in diagnosing psychological disorders in adults.
Demonstrate competency in clinical assessment techniques.
Apply biopsychosocial approaches to diagnostic case formulation and evidence-based interventions.
Understand the psychosocial and pharmacological management for common psychological disorders.

Understand how to apply ethical principles, cultural considerations when working with diverse populations, and reflective/reflexive practice to formulation and communication of clinical findings in multidisciplinary team settings (relevant to accurate documentation, case report writing, and verbal presentation of case summaries).
Understand the impact of inequitable access to healthcare from a human rights perspective and its implications for working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

Critically engage with the research evidence base to inform clinical decision-making and competence in all (including digital) environments to foster ongoing professional development in psychological practice.

assessment_summary.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_summary.title: Role play: Client Descriptions, Formulations and Reflection

assessment_details.title: Role play: Client Descriptions, Formulations and Reflection
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.task_description: From around Week 5 onwards, you’ll step into the world of clinical practice through a series of six structured role-plays designed to build your clinical competencies in assessment, case formulation, and professional feedback – all in the context of clinical levels of psychopathology. Across the tutorials, you’ll rotate through the roles of clinician (the interviewer), client (the case presenter), and observer (the feedback provider). You will complete each role twice, with every session focusing on a different mental health disorder to give you broad exposure to diverse clinical presentations and challenges. You will be allocated into small groups of three students for these role play sessions. Topics will generally reflect the previous weeks lecture's content, although this may vary. The instructions and responsibilities for each role are outlined in the Course Manual. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The client should submit their case description and formulation via Turnitin on the day of the tutorial.
The observer should submit their observations and reflections before the next tutorial or within one week of the tutorial (which ever is sooner)
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
There are no extensions available for this assessment. If you are unable to complete the assessment item on the due date, please contact your tutor directly.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Written Case Study Report
assessment_details.task_description: For this assessment you will write a professional case report based on a recorded mock clinical interview. The interview will feature a psychologist working with an adult client during an initial assessment. Your task is to analyse the interview and produce a case history, and psychological case formulation including diagnosis and recommendations. Your case report should demonstrate ethical and professional conduct, integrate client experience with psychological theory, and provide evidence of sound clinical judgement. The final product should be concise, coherent, and suitable for inclusion in a client file or for sharing with other professionals. Further information including length and formatting are available in the course manual. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
For full details please review the School of Psychology Student Guidelines - Extensions and Deferred quiz/exam (the web link can be found on the Policies and Procedures tab)
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Indigenous Health Modules and Reflection
assessment_details.task_description: In this task you will complete Modules 1 -3 of the HMBS Indigenous Health Modules (available on Blackboard). After viewing all 3 Modules, you will be asked to complete a reflection response. The reflection question and information about length and formatting are available in the course manual. assessment_details.title: Exam
assessment_details.task_description: A 2-hour exam consisting of questions relevant to each of major lecture topics: mood disorders, anxiety and obsessive compulsive disorders, psychotic disorders, substance use disorders, trauma and stressor related disorders, eating disorders, psychopharmacology, personality disorders, and/or other topics to be advised. The exam questions will be short answer open-ended and will assess knowledge and understanding of diagnostic classifications, theoretical models, course and basic treatment and management recommendations for adult psychopathology acquired primarily through the workshop program. The exam will be held in the exam period. Practice exam questions are provided during the course and optional multiple choice quizzes will be available after each workshop for you to test your knowledge. Please see the course manual for additional instructions. Further information will be provided about the timing and format of the exam during class time. Read full task description Hide full task description
